title: Images, Layers, Manifests, Tags, Digests, and Registry Identity · Changed-Environment Lab
summary: An independent delivery team receives unfamiliar application constraints and must transfer images, layers, manifests, tags, digests, and registry identity into a new image identity and layer provenance ledger without copying the workshop scenario. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of image identity and layer provenance ledger with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and ev…
objectives: Relate image configuration, root filesystem diff layers, history, chain identity, and a container writable layer.
Distinguish names, repositories, tags, manifest digests, config digests, layer digests, and local image IDs.
Trace registry authentication, manifest-index selection, platform resolution, blob reuse, verification, and unpacking during pull.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Docker Image Config Rootfs Layers
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab release tag moved between two manifest-list digests while linux/amd64 and linux/arm64 resolve to different image configs. Establish immutable identity, layer ownership, registry trust, and pull behavior. Competency probe: Relate image configuration, root filesystem diff layers, history, chain identity, and a container writable layer.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Lab Docker Image Config Rootfs Layers.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for relate image configuration, root filesystem diff layers, history, chain identity, and a container writable layer. For Commit to the mission model · Lab Docker Image Config Rootfs Layers, keep Docker Image Config Rootfs Layers connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Docker Registry Push Retention
steps.instruction:
Append the Docker evidence item headed "  # Evidence: docker-registry-push-retention-n1hi9r". Tailor its image-identity.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run Docker commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Docker Registry Push Retention.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Docker Registry Push Retention into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Docker Registry Push Retention, keep Docker Registry Push Retention connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-registry-push-retention-n1hi9r
  - competency: docker-registry-push-retention
    artifact: image-identity.yaml
    decision: registry push retention
    scenario-variant: 2
    environment: ephemeral development host with constrained disk and memory
    fault-injection: fill the writable layer before graceful termination
    retained-proof: cold-build trace and content-store usage comparison
    case-key: n1hi9r
    change: |
      reference: registry.example.test/status@sha256:verified-digest
      manifest: linux-amd64
      config: content-addressed
      layers: ordered-and-shared
    verify: docker buildx imagetools inspect registry.example.test/status@sha256:verified-digest
    failure: Reject a mutable tag as release identity or a history listing as proof that secrets never entered a layer.
    repair: Resolve the digest, inspect manifest and config metadata, trace layer ownership, and retain provenance for the selected platform.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.
